title: Introduction to pegap
text: RStudio currently extensive packages PE Portfolio Analysis. package aims bridge couple gaps.

title: Introduction to pegap
text: pegap contains pre-built simple dataset practice containing 5 fake funds’ information. dataset contains five variables: - fund — name fund - vintage_year — year fund began investing - commitment — total capital committed USD ($) - `called` — capital called date USD ($) - recallable — recallable distributions USD ($)
code: code_script #>     fund vintage_year commitment called recallable #> 1 Fund A         2019      10000   9000       1000 #> 2 Fund B         2020      15000  12000        500 #> 3 Fund C         2019       8000   6000        500 #> 4 Fund D         2021      20000  10000          0 #> 5 Fund E         2022      12000   3000          0

title: Introduction to pegap
text: Quantifies risk weighting portfolios heavily one year using Herfindahl-Hirschman Index (HHI). HHI scaled 0-1, multiplied 100 get intuitive score. formula also accounts number vintage years portfolio 2 vintages able reach score 100 (wouldn’t normally). Usually, HHI counter-intuitive, low HHI better. Using scoring formula, inverse true: score 100 means perfect diversification across years whereas lower scores represent intense concentrations one years. Note: HHI>0.25, warning message displays.
code: vintage_diversification_score(   fund_names = code_script$fund,   vintage_years = code_script$vintage_year,   committed_capital = code_script$commitment ) #> $vintage_weights #>  2021  2019  2020  2022  #> 0.308 0.277 0.231 0.185  #>  #> $HHI #> [1] 0.259 #>  #> $effective_vintages #> [1] 3.9 #>  #> $diversification_score #> [1] 98.8 #>  #> $flag #> [1] "WARNING: High vintage concentration"

title: Introduction to pegap
text: Forecasts expected capital calls 5-year timeline using realistic preset deployment curve (Year1-Slow, Year3-Peak) calculates fund’s current unfunded liability (commitment - called + recallable) distributes across five years table. table shows total current unfunded liability, shows break appropriately labeled headers. Unfunded liability schedules help inform investors fund managers capital needed (words much investors owe fund manager given year). structure allows investors hold onto assets, liquidating necessary (thus allowing increase personal wealth funds otherwise sitting around nothing). important PE funds typically make investments require longer maturity timelines, meaning reason draw investors’ capital significant transaction occurs. deployment curve defined : Year 1: 5% Year 2: 20% Year 3: 35% Year 4: 25% Year 5: 15%
code: unfunded_liability_schedule(code_script) #>     fund current_unfunded Year1 Year2 Year3 Year4 Year5 #> 1 Fund A             2000   100   400   700   500   300 #> 2 Fund B             3500   175   700  1225   875   525 #> 3 Fund C             2500   125   500   875   625   375 #> 4 Fund D            10000   500  2000  3500  2500  1500 #> 5 Fund E             9000   450  1800  3150  2250  1350

title: Introduction to pegap
text: Provides visualisation portfolio scatterplot. point represents fund, size shows total commitment colour shows deployment progress. x-axis shows vintage year, y-axis shows remaining unfunded commitment. calls upon ggplot2’s theme_classic(), automatic labels x-axis, y-axis, legend (size & colour). colour scale pre-defined : High (75% called): Dark Green Middle (45%-70% called): Orange Low (Less 45% called): Red

title: Authors and Citation
text: Oliver Reece. Author, maintainer.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Reece O (2026). pegap: Private Equity Portfolio Analysis Tools. R package version 0.0.0.9000, https://adc-405-s26.github.io/pegap/.
code: @Manual{,   title = {pegap: Private Equity Portfolio Analysis Tools},   author = {Oliver Reece},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.0.0.9000},   url = {https://adc-405-s26.github.io/pegap/}, }

title: Private Equity Portfolio Analysis Tools
text: pegap R package made bridge gaps private equity portfolio analysis. contains functions calculating vintage year diversification, forecasting unfunded capital call liabilities, visualising portfolios.

title: Private Equity Portfolio Analysis Tools
text: pegap 3 main functions: vintage_diversification_score() — scores concentrated PE portfolio across vintage years using HHI unfunded_liability_schedule() — forecasts future capital calls across 5-year schedule preset drawdown curve portfolio_plot() — visualises portfolio progress vintage year scatterplot Listed () simple examples corresponding outputs:

title: Private Equity Portfolio Analysis Tools
code: library(pegap)  vintage_diversification_score(   fund_names = c("Fund A", "Fund B", "Fund C"),   vintage_years = c(2019, 2020, 2019),   committed_capital = c(10000, 15000, 8000) ) #> $vintage_weights #>  2019  2020  #> 0.545 0.455  #>  #> $HHI #> [1] 0.504 #>  #> $effective_vintages #> [1] 2 #>  #> $diversification_score #> [1] 99.2 #>  #> $flag #> [1] "WARNING: High vintage concentration"  unfunded_liability_schedule(code_script) #>     fund current_unfunded Year1 Year2 Year3 Year4 Year5 #> 1 Fund A             2000   100   400   700   500   300 #> 2 Fund B             3500   175   700  1225   875   525 #> 3 Fund C             2500   125   500   875   625   375 #> 4 Fund D            10000   500  2000  3500  2500  1500 #> 5 Fund E             9000   450  1800  3150  2250  1350  portfolio_plot(code_script)

title: Plot PE Portfolio Information — portfolio_plot
text: Plot PE Portfolio Information

title: Plot PE Portfolio Information — portfolio_plot
text: data data frame containing fund information. Column names default fund, vintage_year, commitment, called, recallable. currency USD.

title: Plot PE Portfolio Information — portfolio_plot
text: ggplot2 scatter plot showing unfunded commitment vintage year, point size scaled commitment colour indicating deployment progress.

title: Plot PE Portfolio Information — portfolio_plot
code: df <- data.frame(   fund = c("Fund A", "Fund B", "Fund C"),   vintage_year = c(2019, 2020, 2021),   commitment = c(20000000, 25000000, 15000000),   called = c(16000000, 14000000, 6000000),   recallable = c(1000000, 500000, 0) ) portfolio_plot(df)

title: Calculate Unfunded PE Liabilities and Create an Appropriate Schedule — unfunded_liability_schedule
Calculate Unfunded PE Liabilities and Create an Appropriate Schedule — unfunded_liability_schedule
text: data data frame containing information fund. Column names default fund, commitment, called, recallable.

title: Calculate Unfunded PE Liabilities and Create an Appropriate Schedule — unfunded_liability_schedule
text: tibble showing current unfunded liabilities 5-year deployment schedule.

title: Calculate Unfunded PE Liabilities and Create an Appropriate Schedule — unfunded_liability_schedule
code: df <- data.frame(   fund = c("Fund A", "Fund B"),   commitment = c(10000000, 15000000),   called = c(4000000, 3000000),   recallable = c(1000000, 500000) ) unfunded_liability_schedule(df) #>     fund current_unfunded  Year1   Year2   Year3   Year4   Year5 #> 1 Fund A          7000000 350000 1400000 2450000 1750000 1050000 #> 2 Fund B         12500000 625000 2500000 4375000 3125000 1875000

text: fund_names character vector fund names vintage_years numeric vector vintage years equal array length fund_names committed_capital numeric vector USD values equal array length fund_names

title: Score Vintage Year Diversification — vintage_diversification_score
text: list containing vintage_weights, HHI, effective_vintages, diversification_score (0-100)

title: Score Vintage Year Diversification — vintage_diversification_score
code: vintage_diversification_score(   c("Fund A", "Fund B", "Fund C"),   c(2018, 2019, 2018),   c(10000000, 15000000, 5000000) ) #> $vintage_weights #> 2018 2019  #>  0.5  0.5  #>  #> $HHI #> [1] 0.5 #>  #> $effective_vintages #> [1] 2 #>  #> $diversification_score #> [1] 100 #>  #> $flag #> [1] "WARNING: High vintage concentration" #>
